name: fpm
description: Effing package management — CLI that builds deb, rpm, pacman, snap, pkg, and more from multiple sources.

seo.title: fpm — CI/CD for Applications
seo.meta_description: fpm: Effing package management — CLI that builds deb, rpm, pacman, snap, pkg, and more from multiple sources.

when_to_use: Build deb, rpm, and pacman packages from single source
Cross-format packaging in CI without format expertise
Package NodeJS apps as system-level deb/rpm installers
Automate multi-distro package releases in one pipeline

pros: Builds deb, rpm, pacman, snap, and more from one CLI
Saves weeks vs learning each distro's packaging
Widely used, stable for over a decade
cons: Won't pass strict distro policy for official repos
Ruby runtime is a dependency to install
Limited support for modern signing workflows
review.verdict: The pragmatic pick for shipping your own deb/rpm without becoming a packaging expert.
review.best_for: Teams shipping third-party apps across distros without targeting official repos.
review.not_for: Apps aiming for inclusion in Debian/Fedora/Ubuntu main archives.

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Free/open-source — no commercial pricing, no HWM.
